Cambodia Through My Eyes

The Art of Doing Nothing – Lakeside Chilling in Cambodia

18/03/2026

Route6_Rider

Found the perfect spot to master the Khmer art of “relaxing.” There’s nothing quite like swinging in a hammock under a thatched roof, cold Angkor Beer in hand, with a gentle breeze blowing off the water. This is where time slows down and the only item on the agenda is a nap.

Tip: Most lakeside huts (Hut/Kiosks) are free to use as long as you order food or drinks. It’s the ultimate budget-friendly luxury!

How to Get Here: > * If you are already in town: For short-distance taxi rides within the city, you can check out PassApp for options.

  • If you are traveling between Phnom Penh and Siem Reap: You can book a VET Bus ticket online via 12Go. They usually stop right at these lakeside huts for a scenic rest break!

Cambodia Through My Eyes

As a blogger living in Cambodia, I hope that from my perspective, you can get a different visual experience. Although it’s not a high-end, polished photo, it can bring you the real feeling of being a foreigner living here. This was my original intention when creating this blog.
